matches.match_rationale: The pain includes staff uncertainty about entitlements — a communication and clarity problem. AI can generate clear, plain-language benefits summaries, FAQ documents, and onboarding materials that explain exactly what staff are entitled to under GSIS, PhilHealth, and any supplemental benefits. This directly addresses the 'staff unsure of what they're entitled to' dimension of the pain.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted benefits communication toolkit that auto-generates personalized entitlement summaries per employment type (full-time, part-time, probationary) and answers the most common questions in plain Filipino/English, reducing HR inquiry load.

matches.match_rationale: Staff frequently don't know what benefits they are entitled to, and HR teams spend significant time fielding repetitive entitlement questions. A RAG system grounded in the school's benefits documents, GSIS regulations, and labor code provisions can answer specific staff queries accurately and instantly, reducing HR burden and improving staff confidence. This directly addresses the opacity dimension of the pain.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A staff-facing benefits Q&A chatbot that answers questions like 'Am I eligible for maternity leave top-up?' or 'What does my PhilHealth cover for dependents?' by retrieving from uploaded policy and regulatory documents.

matches.match_rationale: Benefits opacity is an ongoing, multi-turn problem — staff need to ask follow-up questions, get personalized answers based on their employment status, and understand implications of different choices (e.g., HMO tier selection). A conversational agent can handle this complexity across multiple turns, maintaining context about the employee's situation, unlike static FAQs or one-shot RAG responses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An HR virtual assistant that guides staff through benefits enrollment, answers multi-turn eligibility questions, explains the gap versus corporate competitors, and flags when a staff member may be under-utilizing entitlements they already have.

matches.match_rationale: The school is at a competitive disadvantage without knowing what peer institutions (corporate, international schools) offer. An AI research agent can autonomously gather and synthesize competitive benefits benchmarking data — scraping job postings, analyzing publicly available compensation surveys, and summarizing the gap. This gives leadership the evidence base needed to make the case for benefits investment.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A competitive benefits benchmarking agent that continuously monitors job postings from rival schools and corporate employers, extracts stated benefits, and produces a quarterly gap report showing where the operator is losing the recruitment battle.

matches.match_rationale: The pain explicitly mentions retention challenges and school-hopping. With payroll, tenure, and exit interview data, a propensity model can identify which staff are at highest attrition risk due to benefits dissatisfaction — enabling targeted retention interventions before the resignation is submitted. This converts a passive benefits problem into a proactive retention tool.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A staff attrition risk model that scores employees monthly on flight-risk probability, factoring in tenure, dependents, salary band, benefits uptake patterns, and peer benchmarks — surfacing at-risk staff to HR before they resign.
